skill.name: debugging-toolkit-smart-debug
skill.description: Guide structured debugging with triage, observability review, hypothesis testing, fix planning, and validation.
skill.summary: Structured AI-assisted workflow for diagnosing and validating software issues.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static backtick findings at SKILL.md:21 and SKILL.md:145 are Markdown inline code and a TypeScript example fence, not shell execution. The weak-crypto flags at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:92 and the network reconnaissance flag at SKILL.md:132 are text-only false positives; no executable code, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or network behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Complex production and development bugs are hard to triage without a repeatable process. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through evidence collection, hypothesis ranking, fix planning, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Structures initial bug triage from errors, stack traces, reproduction steps, components, and environment details.
Guides collection of logs, traces, APM metrics, error tracking data, and deployment timeline evidence.
Generates ranked debugging hypotheses with supporting evidence, falsification criteria, and test approaches.
Recommends debugging strategies such as interactive debugging, observability review, time-travel debugging, and statistical comparison.
Defines validation steps, regression tests, monitoring updates, and prevention actions after a fix.
limitations: It does not include executable integrations for Sentry, DataDog, New Relic, or other observability platforms.
It depends on user-provided logs, traces, code context, and reproduction details.
The TypeScript section is an illustrative example, not a runnable implementation.
Production debugging steps still require proper access control, privacy review, and operational approval.

use_cases.title: Triage Production Incidents
use_cases.description: Guide incident responders from symptoms to ranked hypotheses using logs, traces, metrics, and deployment history.

use_cases.title: Debug Application Regressions
use_cases.description: Help developers compare expected and failing behavior, identify likely code paths, and plan focused validation.

use_cases.title: Create Post-Fix Prevention Plans
use_cases.description: Turn a confirmed root cause into regression tests, monitoring updates, runbook notes, and rollback checks.

prompt_templates.title: Start Basic Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has an error message and needs a first debugging plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this debugging skill to triage this issue. Error: [paste error]. Context: [component and environment]. Provide likely causes, missing information, and first checks.

prompt_templates.title: Rank Root Cause Hypotheses
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer has logs, traces, and reproduction notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this debugging workflow to analyze the provided evidence. Rank 3 to 5 root cause hypotheses with confidence, supporting evidence, falsification steps, and next tests.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production-Safe Debugging
prompt_templates.scenario: A team needs to investigate a production issue without increasing risk.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production-safe debugging plan for this issue. Include observability queries, safe instrumentation, sampling, access controls, validation signals, and rollback criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Prevent Recurrence
prompt_templates.scenario: A fix is ready and needs a complete verification plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed fix using the skill. Produce a validation plan with test coverage, performance comparison, canary checks, monitoring updates, and recurrence prevention steps.

output_examples.input: Checkout timeouts affect about five percent of users in production after the latest release.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce an issue summary, ranked hypotheses, observability queries, a focused validation plan, and prevention actions.

output_examples.input: A local test fails intermittently with a state mismatch after async updates.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest reproduction controls, timing checks, breakpoint locations, likely race conditions, and regression tests.

output_examples.input: An API integration started returning authentication errors in staging only.
output_examples.output: The skill would compare environment configuration, deployment changes, dependency behavior, error trends, and verification steps.

best_practices: Provide exact errors, stack traces, reproduction steps, affected components, and environment details before asking for analysis.
Validate each hypothesis with observable evidence before changing production systems.
Add regression tests, monitoring, and runbook notes after the root cause is confirmed.
anti_patterns: Do not apply a fix before collecting enough evidence to explain the failure.
Do not add broad production logging without privacy, cost, and performance review.
Do not treat the illustrative TypeScript example as an installed integration.

faq.question: Does this skill run debugging tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured workflow and prompts for analysis. Users must run tools and provide outputs.

faq.question: Can it help with production incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides production-safe evidence collection, hypothesis ranking, validation signals, and prevention planning.

faq.question: Which observability tools does it mention?
faq.answer: It references tools such as Sentry, DataDog, New Relic, Dynatrace, Jaeger, Zipkin, Honeycomb, ELK, Splunk, and Loki.

faq.question: Is there executable code in the skill?
faq.answer: No. The only code-like section is a TypeScript-style example used to explain a debugging session.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: The best input includes errors, stack traces, logs, traces, affected services, reproduction steps, and recent deployment changes.

faq.question: Can it generate a final fix?
faq.answer: It can help plan and review a fix, but the actual implementation depends on the project code and test results.
